- claim hot-path-5d642d66d50f-claim-6 [ACTIVE] current_metric_value family=current_metric_value: The user spent around $60 on an Instacart grocery order on 2023-02-28.; scope=User grocery spending benchmark for an Instacart order on 2023-02-28; context="The user mentioned this spending while discussing grocery shopping and meal planning."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:810b1e88362e07a514dc5389d81ecf3d quote="spent around $60 today"

- claim hot-path-3a7c52577962-claim-12 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user was thinking of using the $100 monthly FarmFresh savings to try new recipes and ingredients at FreshMarket.; scope=User's grocery exploration plan from the imported past chat session dated 2023-01-05.; context="The user described FreshMarket as a new grocery store they had discovered and wanted advice on navigating it and finding deals."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:1719e562c771dff44869df8138d8cd1e quote="I'm actually thinking of using the $100 to try out some new recipes and ingredients at FreshMarket"

FreshMarket appealed because of its selection of international ingredients, and suggested shopping strategy included touring the store, asking staff, checking signs/digital coupons, watching sale cycles, considering bulk discounts, and exploring the international aisle for specialty ingredients.; scope=User grocery exploration plan involving FreshMarket and international ingredients; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:1719e562c771dff44869df8138d8cd1e quote="using the $100 to try out some new recipes and ingredients at FreshMarket, the new grocery store I discovered last month"

The savings were framed as part of becoming more mindful about spending and planning meals more efficiently.; scope=User grocery spending and meal-planning context involving FarmFresh cancellation; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:1719e562c771dff44869df8138d8cd1e quote="I cancelled my monthly grocery delivery subscription from FarmFresh, which I wasn't using frequently enough, and saved $100 per month."

LongMemEval past chat session.
Session date: 2023/02/28 (Tue) 11:34
Transcript:
user: I'm planning to make some new recipes this week and I need some inspiration. Can you suggest some healthy dinner ideas that incorporate chicken breasts and ground beef? By the way, I just did an online grocery order from Instacart and had it delivered to my doorstep, and spent around $60 today.
assistant: Instacart is so convenient, isn't it? Congrats on getting your groceries delivered!...
...user: I'm also thinking of making some changes to my grocery shopping routine. Do you have any suggestions on how I can optimize my grocery lists and reduce food waste?
assistant: Optimizing your grocery lists and reducing food waste are excellent goals! Here are some suggestions to help you achieve them:

**1. Plan your meals and make a grocery list:**
Before you shop, plan out your meals for the week. This will help you create a focused grocery list and avoid buying unnecessary items. You can use a meal planning app, a physical planner, or even a note-taking app on your phone....
...Group your grocery list by store sections (e.g., produce, meat, dairy, canned goods, etc.). This will help you navigate the store more efficiently and reduce impulse buys....
...**6. Avoid shopping when you're hungry:**
Shopping for groceries on a full stomach can help you avoid impulse buys and stick to your list.

**7. Use cashback apps and digital coupons:**
Apps like Ibotta, Fetch Rewards, and Checkout 51 offer cashback on certain grocery items. Digital coupons can also help you save money and reduce waste.

**8. Store food properly:**
Make sure you're storing your groceries properly to extend their shelf life. Check expiration dates, keep your fridge and pantry organized, and use the "first in, first out" rule....
...Regularly review your grocery lists and shopping habits to identify areas for improvement. Adjust your strategies as needed to optimize your shopping routine and reduce waste.

**Additional tips:**

* Consider shopping at local farmers' markets or through community-supported agriculture (CSA) programs to reduce packaging waste and support local farmers....
...By implementing these strategies, you can optimize your grocery lists, reduce food waste, and make a positive impact on the environment. Happy shopping!...
...Consider using a pantry inventory app like StillTasty, FreshBox, or Pantry Check. These apps allow you to track the items you have, their expiration dates, and even provide recipe suggestions based on what you have on hand.

**2. Spreadsheets or Notes App:**
Create a spreadsheet or use a notes app like Evernote or OneNote to track your pantry and fridge inventory. Set up columns or sections for categories like "Pantry," "Fridge," "Freezer," and "Expiration Dates." Update the list whenever you add or remove items....
...Use a "first in, first out" system to ensure you use older items before they expire. Check expiration dates regularly and plan meals around items that are approaching their expiration dates....
...* **Expiration Date**: The expiration date of the item (if applicable)...
...**4. Update Regularly:**
Regularly update your spreadsheet when you add or remove items from your pantry and fridge. This will help you maintain an accurate inventory and avoid duplication....
...Make sure your spreadsheet is accessible and easy to use on your mobile devices. This will allow you to update your inventory and shopping list on-the-go....
...The column to note if the item is a staple or a one-time purchase is also a fantastic idea. This will help you identify which items you need to restock regularly and which ones are single-use purchases. This information will be super useful when planning your shopping trips and creating your grocery lists....
...If you have any more questions or need further assistance, feel free to ask. Otherwise, happy inventory tracking, and I hope your new system helps you stay organized and save t
[truncated 39 chars]

...3. **Labeling and Dating:** Label each container or bag with the meal name, date, and any reheating instructions. This ensures you know what you have and how long it's been stored....
...* **Freezing:** If you don't plan to consume your meals within 3-4 days, consider freezing them for up to 3 months. Label and date the containers or bags, and store them in the freezer. When reheating, thaw overnight in the refrigerator or reheat from frozen in the microwave or oven....
...user: I'm thinking of organizing my pantry and kitchen cabinets to make meal prep more efficient. Do you have any suggestions on how to categorize and store my groceries, especially for dry goods like rice, pasta, and canned goods?
assistant: Pantry and kitchen cabinet organization is a great step in making meal prep more efficient!

Here are some suggestions on how to categorize and store your groceries, especially for dry goods like rice, pasta, and canned goods:...
...2. **Labeling:** Label each container or bin with its contents and the date you stored them....
...user: I've been meaning to organize my pantry and kitchen cabinets for a while now, and your suggestions are super helpful. I'm excited to get started this weekend. By the way, I'm glad I cancelled my monthly grocery delivery subscription from FarmFresh, which I wasn't using frequently enough, and saved $100 per month. It's great to be more mindful of my spending and plan my meals more efficiently.
assistant: I'm thrilled to hear that! Canceling the subscription was a great decision, and saving $100 per month is a significant amount that can be put towards more important things....
...user: I'm actually thinking of using the $100 to try out some new recipes and ingredients at FreshMarket, the new grocery store I discovered last month. I was impressed with their selection of international ingredients, and I want to explore more of their offerings. Do you have any suggestions on how to navigate their store or find the best deals?...
...4. **Shop the Perimeter:** Focus on shopping the perimeter of the store, where you'll often find fresh produce, meats, and dairy products. These items tend to be healthier and more affordable than processed foods....
...3. **Food Blogs:** Explore food blogs or websites that focus on international cuisine or specific ingredients. You can find plenty of recipe ideas and cooking tips online....

systemPrompts:
You are an objective evaluator for a long-term-memory benchmark.
Decide whether the retrieved memory context is sufficient to answer the question in the same core direction as the noisy expected-answer label.
Retrieved memory context is the only source of truth. The expected-answer label is a comparison hint, not evidence.
Mark supported=true when the context contains equivalent source-grounded facts, preferences, events, or rationale, even if wording differs.
For imported chat transcripts, source-grounded support may come from adjacent turns and the active topic, not only from one sentence that literally repeats the answer.
Accept a strong conversational entailment when the expected answer follows from the surrounding user/assistant turns and no other retrieved source points to a competing answer.
Details that appear only in the expected-answer label but not in retrieved memory are non-binding and must not be called central unless the retrieved context itself makes them necessary for answering the question.
The official expected answer can contain noisy illustrative examples or broad inferences not literally present in the source. Do not require every illustrative example when the memory supports the central answer.
For preference or personalization questions, supported=true when the retrieved memory contains the concrete user-specific consideration needed to personalize the answer in the same core direction, even if the expected answer lists additional absent examples.
Do not mark unsupported solely because the official answer names an extra participant, pet name, or causal detail that is absent from the retrieved source while the central remembered event, preference, count, or rationale is still supported.
Mark supported=false when the context is missing the central answer, contradicts it, leaves several plausible competing answers, or only supports a weaker unrelated subset.
Return only the configured JSON object.

structured.fullText: {"hypothesis":"54 days passed between the FarmFresh cancellation on 2023-01-05 and the Instacart online grocery order on 2023-02-28.","reason":"The memory gives the FarmFresh cancellation in the 2023-01-05 session and the Instacart order in the 2023-02-28 session, which are 54 days apart.","supported":true}
